commands.review_summary:
Adds `channels.slack.dm.collapseAssistantThreads`, gates Slack assistant DM thread session routing on it, and updates Slack docs plus a routing regression test.

PR surface: Source +18, Tests +36, Docs +2. Total +56 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Not end to end: current source and tests show assistant-thread timestamps create suffixed DM sessions, but this PR has no real Slack Agent/Assistant workspace proof.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A maintainer or contributor needs to patch the config contract and provide real Slack proof; ClawSweeper automation cannot supply the contributor's workspace evidence.

target.title: fix(slack): opt-in flag to collapse assistant DM threads into base DM session
target.body:
## Summary

Adds `channels.slack.dm.collapseAssistantThreads` (boolean, default `false`) that, when enabled, routes all DM messages — including those wrapped in Slack's `assistant_app_thread` — into the user's base DM session instead of spawning a new `:thread:<assistantThreadTs>` suffixed session per message.

## Why

When the Slack app has the "Agent or Assistant" capability enabled, every inbound DM carries a unique `assistant_app_thread.thread_ts`. The current routing in `prepare-routing.ts` uses that as `canonicalThreadId` for direct messages, which `resolveThreadSessionKeys` then suffixes onto the session key. Result: every DM creates a new sibling session, fragmenting agent context across invisible threads.

Per ClawSweeper's triage on #87019, the existing contract is documented and tested, so changing the default behavior is a product decision. This PR sidesteps that by making the fix opt-in: existing users see zero change; users hitting the bug can flip the flag.

## Changes

- New config field `channels.slack.dm.collapseAssistantThreads` (default `false`)
- `prepare-routing.ts` gates the `isDirectMessage → assistantThreadId` path on the flag
- New regression test in `prepare.thread-session-key.test.ts` asserting flag=true collapses the session key
- Docs update in `docs/channels/slack.md`

## Verification

- Existing test (`assistant DM with assistantThreadTs produces :thread:<ts>` session key) still passes — default behavior unchanged
- New test asserts flag=true produces base DM session key
- Type-check passes for the slack extension

Fixes #87019
target.author: paulgomega
